channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Grupo Concentra ficha a Manu Alba Ruiz como Head of Retail para España
item.description: Grupo Concentra sigue incorporando profesionales de Generali; el viernes se ha conocido que ha sumado a su proyecto a Manu Alba Ruiz, como Head of Retail para España . Experto en el desarrollo y gestión de redes de ventas, tanto en entornos B2B como B2C , en la identificación de nuevas oportunidades de negocio, captación de cuentas clave y gestión activa de la cartera de clientes, hasta ahora era responsable del Canal Agentes de la Territorial Centro de Generali , grupo en el que llevaba casi 13 años. Licenciado en Comunicación Audiovisual, antes ha trabajado también en Seguros Bilbao.

item.title: Mapfre, empresa más atractiva para trabajar en el sector asegurador
item.description: Mapfre ha sido reconocida como la empresa más atractiva para trabajar en el sector asegurador, según la XVII edición del informe Randstad Employer Brand Research, elaborado a partir de más de 7.800 encuestas en España. En esta edición, junto a Mapfre , han sido reconocidas como empresas más atractivas en sus respectivos sectores Atresmedia, BBVA, IBM, ID Logistics, IKEA, Inditex, Konecta, KPMG, L’Oréal, Meliá Hotels International, Mercedes-Benz, Nestlé, Roche Farma, Schneider Electric, Siemens Energy y Telefónica . El informe identifica como principales factores de elección de empleo el salario y los beneficios, el ambiente laboral, el equilibrio entre vida personal y profesional, la seguridad laboral y las oportunidades de desarrollo.

item.title: Cerrajero y seguro de hogar: cuándo cubre la póliza (y cuándo no) la apertura o el cambio de cerradura
item.description: Si la pérdida no genera un riesgo claro para la seguridad, el seguro puede limitarse a cubrir la apertura y dejar fuera el cambio completo de cerradura. Sin embargo, no todas las actuaciones del cerrajero quedan cubiertas por el seguro de hogar . Cuando el asegurado pierde las llaves de la vivienda, la póliza puede cubrir la apertura de la puerta y, en algunos contratos, la sustitución del bombín .

item.title: California Governor Seeks Extension of FEMA Funding for LA Wildfire Victims
item.description: Newsom’s request follows months of coordination by Cal OES with FEMA to assess wildfire survivors’ needs. Gavin Newsom is asking the federal government extend disaster assistance programs for thousands of victims of the January 2025 Los Angeles wildfires who are still working to rebuild. Newsom is requesting a 12-month extension of the Federal Emergency Management Agency’s Individuals and Households Program which provides financial and housing assistance to disaster survivors.

item.title: Mercedes-Benz Recalling 144,000 US Vehicles for Display Issues
item.description: Mercedes-Benz is recalling 144,049 vehicles in the U.S. Related: Tesla Recalling 219,000 US Vehicles for Rearview Image Issue The infotainment control unit, which is responsible for navigation and multimedia features, may reset while the vehicle is in motion and cause the instrument panel display to go blank, the regulator said. over instrument panel display issues, the U.S.

item.title: vrs Adjusters takes next step in international growth with Malaysia tie-up
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sedgwick | Investing in people is shaping the future of loss adjusting in Asia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Hannover Re grew premium by 18.8% during 1.4 renewals
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending May 10th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending May 10th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending May 10th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Hannover Re grows, shares few losses with ILS, says price pressures have upper hand in P&C
item.description: Hannover Re reported a 48% increase in group profit for the first-quarter of 2026, revealing renewals growth of 5.6% for the year so far, while acknowledging that price pressures have the upper hand in property and casualty reinsurance. Thanks to that, Hannover Re has reported only sharing EUR 17 million of its large losses with insurance-linked securities (ILS) capital partners in the period, a significantly lower figure than in the prior year Q1. Recall that, in the first-quarter of 2025, Hannover Re ceded EUR 438 million of its large natural catastrophe losses with insurance-linked securities (ILS) capital sources , with the California wildfires the main natural event that drove cessions to its capital partners.

item.title: Florida Citizens now targets up to $600m of reinsurance from Everglades Re II 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: Florida Citizens returned to the market in April, initially looking to secure $450 million of named storm reinsurance protection from this new Everglades Re II 2026-1 cat bond deal. if offering investors three tranches of Series 2026-1 cat bond notes to provide Florida Citizens with multi-year annual aggregate named storm reinsurance covering the state of Florida, on an indemnity trigger basis, across a three year term and three risk periods. Interestingly, $600 million was a figure that had been discussed as the maximum Florida Citizens was likely to secure in 2026 from the cat bond market as its reinsurance needs have declined alongside its steady reduction in policies-in-force and exposure, in advance of the hurricane season.

item.title: Moody’s maintains stable outlook for European insurers amid economic and geopolitical pressures
item.description: Moody’s Ratings, the international credit ratings, research and risk analysis company, has reaffirmed its stable outlook for Europe’s property and casualty (P&C) and life insurance sectors, while highlighting growing pressure from competition, slower economic growth and geopolitical uncertainty. Moody’s said profitability among P&C insurers has recovered to levels recorded before the COVID-19 pandemic, largely due to premium increases introduced across European markets over the past year. Moody’s stated that its central forecast assumes the conflict in the Middle East will have only a contained impact on European insurers.

item.title: Aon Q1’26 market insights reports softer pricing with select areas of hardening
item.description: Aon, a global professional services firm focused on risk, retirement and health solutions, reports in its Q1 2026 Global Insurance Market Insights that the insurance sector is experiencing broadly softer pricing conditions, supported by strong capacity and intensifying competition, alongside continued discipline in specific higher-risk and regulated areas. However, Aon reports that the pace of reductions is slowing in several areas, with signs of stabilisation in lines such as directors and officers insurance and cyber. Aon notes that pricing is falling across most regions and lines of business as insurers compete more aggressively and deploy increased capacity, with some large US property accounts seeing double-digit reductions.

item.title: Kestrel grows revenue in Q1’26 despite net loss
item.description: Kestrel’s fee revenue in Q1 2026, a key earnings driver for many fronting carriers, rose to $3.12 million from $807,000 in the same period of 2025. Commenting on the results, Kestrel’s Chief Executive Officer, Luke Ledbetter, said, “The first quarter demonstrated continued momentum in our Program Services segment, with fee revenue and premiums produced up materially year-over-year. Kestrel Group reported sharply higher total revenues of $10.2 million for Q1 2026 as the specialty insurance platform expanded its fronting operations and investment income.

item.title: Ships’ Signals Go Haywire as Hormuz Strait Tensions Escalate
item.description: “Shipping, and most noticeably AIS data, got caught in the crossfire,” he said, referring to the Automatic Identification System that uses radio signals to broadcast ships’ real-time locations. Increased levels of signal jamming can complicate analyses of real-time traffic data in the Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz, which is crucial to understanding energy flows. Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained at a near halt on Monday, with two tankers making rare crossings to emerge in the Gulf of Oman.

item.title: Seoul Condemns Strike on Vessel in Strait of Hormuz as Probe Continues
item.description: South Korea strongly condemned what it described as an attack on a civilian cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz after a government investigation found the ship was struck by unidentified objects, though it stopped short of assigning blame. A South Korean government joint investigation found that two “unidentified aerial objects” struck the stern of the HMM Namu about one minute apart on May 4 local time, while the vessel was anchored near the United Arab Emirates side of the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Foreign Ministry. The findings raise pressure on Seoul as the US pushes allies to contribute more to maritime security efforts around the Strait of Hormuz following escalating tensions tied to the conflict in Iran.

item.title: Hedge Funds Make Their Move as Litigation Finance Assets Slump
item.description: A losing streak in litigation finance is attracting hedge funds and other alternative investment managers looking to invest in legal claims at distressed valuations, according to people familiar with the matter. Litigation finance has doubled in size over the past decade to become a $20 billion industry, channeling capital into lawsuits spanning everything from allegations of corporate malfeasance to bankruptcy disputes. In litigation finance, an investor provides capital to support a legal claim, typically by covering legal fees or other costs as a case moves through the courts.

item.title: FEMA Council Backs Overhaul of Disaster Response
item.description: In an executive order last year, Trump established the FEMA Review Council to propose an overhaul of the nation’s disaster-response system. The recommendations, finalized at a council meeting in Washington, include reassessing FEMA staffing, rethinking what disasters qualify for federal disaster aid and then streamlining its distribution, reforming the National Flood Insurance Program, and shifting more training and spending decisions to state, local and tribal authorities, over a period of two to three years. This is a pivotal moment — not only for FEMA, but for every state, local, tribal, and territorial emergency manager who depends on a strong federal partnership before, during, and after disasters,” said Carrie Speranza, former chair of FEMA National Advisory Council, a now-disbanded group of experts that advised the agency on all aspects of emergency management.

item.title: ‘Expectation vs. experience is the opportunity’: Vietnam’s TCGIns doubles down on AI, health, SME insurance
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Principal integrates with Employee Navigator for EOI enrollment
item.description: Principal Financial Group announced a new integration with Employee Navigator aimed at streamlining evidence of insurability (EOI) during employee benefits enrollment. The API-based integration allows employees to complete required EOI health questions directly within the enrollment experience on Employee Navigator, helping reduce administrative steps and speed up underwriting decisions for group benefits. According to Principal, the integration is designed to minimize enrollment disruptions for employees, reduce administrative touchpoints for employers and brokers, and accelerate underwriting reviews.

item.title: New: The Hartford Aging Insights Team
item.description: The Hartford filed a trademark application for “The Hartford Aging Insights Team” on May 6, 2026. The application covers a broad range of services focused on the over 50 market, including insurance and financial consulting, original research, educational programs, and technology consulting.

item.title: New: ONE ENTRY. ONE EXPERIENCE. ZERO GUESSING.
item.description: ZERO GUESSING.” on May 6, 2026, according to USPTO records. Penn Mutual filed a trademark application for “ONE ENTRY. The filing was submitted by The Penn Mutual Life Insurance Company and is currently listed under “New Application – Record Initialized Not Assigned To Examiner.” The post New: ONE ENTRY.

item.title: DUAL UK expands social care insurance offering
item.description: DUAL expanded its social care insurance appetite with an enhanced package targeting children’s homes and Special Educational Needs (SEN) schools. DUAL said the offering was designed for both new and established providers operating in a heavily regulated sector facing rising demand and increasingly complex care needs. The product is underwritten by DUAL UK’s social care team, led by Managing Director James Ramira, who has more than 20 years of experience in the sector.

item.title: MSIG Australia hires Doron Grossman as claims manager for partner distribution
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Logistics giant DP World launches cargo war risk coverage for Middle East trade
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aviso expands Western Australia business with acquisition of Wray Insurance Services
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Inszone acquires Arizona-based Coriano Insurance Agency
item.description: Inszone Insurance has acquired Arizona-based Coriano Insurance Agency, expanding its presence in the Southwest. “We had spoken to several other brokerages, but Inszone stood out,” said Chris Coriano, co-founder of Coriano Insurance Agency. Founded in 2017 by Chris and Emily Coriano, the Phoenix-based agency grew to more than $9 million in premiums through organic growth and referrals.

item.title: Kin Secures $335 Million Catastrophe Bond
item.description: Kin Insurance (Chicago) has completed its largest-ever catastrophe bond, a $335 million transaction through Hestia Re Ltd. The transaction, Kin’s fourth catastrophe bond placement, includes four bonds and expands the company’s storm coverage beyond Florida for the first time to include additional states where Kin operates. “This is our fourth catastrophe bond, and with each one, the terms improve while investor demand grows,” comments Sean Harper, Founder and CEO, Kin.

item.title: Cyber’s promise facing a confidence gap?
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Gallagher launches AI-powered risk scoring framework
item.description: Gallagher launched Gallagher Blueprint, a framework that combines AI analytics, proprietary data, and broker expertise to help clients evaluate and improve their insurance programs. According to the company, Gallagher Blueprint builds on its existing sales methodology by aligning insurance strategy, risk management priorities, and budget into a customized action plan. The offering generates a proprietary Risk Profile Score that compares a client’s risk and insurance program against peer benchmarks and best practices.

item.title: Agentic AI Blurring Core System Boundaries—Datos Insights’ Wein
item.description: Datos Insights (Boston) is seeing the early signs of a major shift in insurance technology architecture, as agentic AI begins to challenge long-standing assumptions about where underwriting, policy administration and other core-system functions should reside, according to Mitch Wein, Head of Insurance Strategy and Advisory, Datos Insights. “The core system vendors are probably in trouble,” Wein comments. Mitch Wein, Head of Insurance Strategy and Advisory, Datos Insights.

item.title: Corgi Raises $160 Million Series B
item.description: (Image source: Corgi website.) Corgi Insurance (San Francisco) has raised $160 million in Series B funding at a $1.3 billion valuation, bringing the company’s total funding to more than $268 million. Nico Laqua, Co-Founder, CEO, Corgi The funding follows Corgi’s previously announced $108 million across its seed and Series A rounds. “We will for sure always be the most passionate, genuine, curious, and ambitious of any company.” Corgi Raises $108M, Launches AI-Driven Full-Stack Carrier for Startups

item.title: Safety Agency Opens Probe into Startup Avride’s Autonomous Crashes in Texas
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said Friday it is opening an investigation into a series of crashes involving startup Avride’s autonomous vehicles that raised questions about their performance. Avride said it welcomes “the opportunity to provide the agency with a deeper understanding of our safety protocols and technology.” It added that in all cases, “the vehicle was under the supervision of a trained safety operator on board. NHTSA said the vehicles’ behavior may indicate excessive assertiveness and insufficient capability, which “may also constitute traffic safety violations.” Some of the crashes resulted in property damage, and one reported minor injury.

item.title: Wisconsin Recycling Firm Posts Unusual Speed Limit Sign
item.description: Outagamie County Recycling & Solid Waste highlighted a new speed limit sign that reads 17.3 MPH on a recent Facebook post. The Wisconsin recycler posted the sign with an odd speed limit to get drivers’ attention. And most importantly, it breaks that ‘autopilot’ feeling we can all fall into when driving familiar routes,” the social media post stated.

item.title: Platform Breach Downs Nearly 9,000 Colleges, Universities in the U.S.
item.description: Nearly 9,000 colleges and universities were impacted by the Canvas outage, which occurred on Thursday evening. The platform used by educational facilities across the U.S. An update issued today indicated the breach had been cleared and user accessibility to Canvas restored.

item.title: Legal challenges to motor finance compensation scheme - update for firms and consumers
item.description: Following the legal challenges to our motor finance compensation scheme, we are setting out further advice for firms and consumers. Our priorities remain to secure fair compensation for consumers as quickly as possible and ensure a healthy motor finance market. Firms should continue to prepare for the scheme until we communicate otherwise.

item.title: Convicted money launderer sentenced to extra prison time
item.description: A convicted money launderer has been sentenced to an additional 499 days in prison for failing to fully pay the money owed under a Confiscation Order. In 2021, Richard Faithfull, now 36, was sentenced to 5 years and 10 months in prison for laundering £2.5 million, following a prosecution brought by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA). When he was originally sentenced, the Judge remarked that Mr Faithfull’s was 'serious offending' linked to the 'human misery caused by boiler room fraud' and that 'money coming in (to accounts controlled by Faithfull) was not being invested, it was simply being slaughtered'.

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Enterprise AI, Space Tech And Biotech Top The Ranks
item.description: This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here . Another week, another infusion of big AI rounds.

item.title: UCITS cat bond fund assets rise 6.5% YTD in 2026, near $20.5bn after April
item.description: Catastrophe bond funds in the UCITS format added over $650 million in assets just in the month of April, as the combined assets under management (AUM) of the UCITS cat bond fund sector reached almost $20.5 billion, having now expanded by 6.5% so far in 2026. The expansion continued in the first-quarter, with UCITS cat bond fund assets growing to over $20 billion for the first time ever in February, but then declining slightly to around $19.8 billion by the end of March 2026. Analyse UCITS catastrophe bond fund assets under management using these charts (data kindly shared by our partner Plenum Investments AG, a specialist insurance-linked securities (ILS) fund manager).

item.title: Swiss Re secures $250m Matterhorn Re 2026-2 retro cat bond at low-end pricing
item.description: This marks the reinsurer’s second catastrophe bond sponsorship under Matterhorn Re of 2026 so far, having secured $150 million of annual aggregate retro reinsurance from a Matterhorn Re 2026-1 cat bond in February. With these new cat bond notes now priced and the coverage secured once the deal settles, Matterhorn Re will now issue a $150 million tranche of Series 2026-2 Class A notes that will provide named storm retrocession across northeast US states only for Swiss Re, over a two hurricane season term with maturity slated for December 2027. Swiss Re ventured back to the cat bond market in April , to sponsor what will now become the sixteenth takedown under its Bermuda-based Matterhorn Re catastrophe bond program.

item.title: 76% of reinsurers delivered double-digit capital growth in 2025: Gallagher Re
item.description: According to Gallagher Re’s 2025 Reinsurance Market Report, roughly 76% of reinsurers within its cohort posted double-digit capital growth in 2025, underscoring another year of strong balance sheet expansion across the sector. Gallagher Re added that foreign exchange movements provided a significant boost to capital growth for non-USD reporting reinsurers, as local currencies strengthened against the US dollar during 2025. The report also highlighted the breadth of capital growth across the industry, with 76% of the reinsurer cohort recording double-digit increases during the year.

item.title: Palomar reports improved reinsurance terms and expands catastrophe bond protection in Q1’26
item.description: Mac Armstrong, the CEO of Palomar Holdings, a US-based speciality insurance company focused on property and casualty coverage, said the firm’s 2026 reinsurance renewals delivered stronger economic terms across both property and casualty business lines, while the company also increased its catastrophe protection through the latest Torrey Pines Re bond issuance. A testament to the performance of these casualty lines.” Palomar Holdings recently completed its seventh Torrey Pines Re catastrophe bond transaction, which provides $410 million of fully collateralised multi-year reinsurance protection covering California earthquake exposure and, for the first time, standalone Hawaii hurricane risk. Speaking during the company’s Q1 2026 earnings call, Armstrong revealed that Palomar completed six reinsurance placements during the quarter, all on improved terms compared with previous arrangements.

item.title: Sompo Re adds Beatrice Morley as Head of London, Reinsurance
item.description: Sompo Re, the reinsurance arm of the global provider of property, casualty, and specialty re/insurance, Sompo, has appointed Beatrice Morley as Head of London, Reinsurance. In her new role, Morley will lead Sompo’s underwriting team in London and work closely with regional leadership to coordinate strategy and local execution. At Aspen, Morley held various roles, including serving as the Global Head of Casualty and Head of International Reinsurance, Head of International and Casualty of Aspen Re, and Head of Asia Pacific and Casualty Treaty for the region.

item.title: US Opens Probe Into Start-Up Avride Self-Driving Crashes in Texas
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said Friday it is opening an investigation in a series of crashes of start-up Avride autonomous vehicles that raised questions about the competency of the vehicles. NHTSA noted that Avride is operating some of its AVs on the Uber UBER.N platform and has offered passenger service to the public since December in Dallas, where many of the crashes occurred. NHTSA said the vehicles’ performance may indicate inappropriate assertiveness and insufficient competence “and may also constitute traffic safety violations,” and added some crashes resulted in property damage and one reported minor injury.

item.title: People Moves: Novatae Names Voorhees as Managing Director of Personal Lines
item.description: Novatae Risk Group , the wholesale insurance business of World Insurance Associates, announced that Brian Voorhees will be joining the company as its new managing director of personal lines. In this role, Voorhees will lead Novatae’s personal lines with responsibility for setting strategic direction, driving growth and delivering strong operational and financial performance. Prior to that, Voorhees served as vice president of business development at NatGen Premier, managing a $750 million portfolio and helping launch the organization’s mass affluent and high-net-worth segments.

item.title: Fertilizer Firms See Profit Windfall as War Upends Supplies
item.description: Fertilizer makers are seeing a windfall from Iran’s war, highlighting the impact of the conflict’s upheaval on supply chains for the key crop nutrients. Elevated fertilizer prices have intensified scrutiny on the industry, which is controlled by only a handful of companies in the US. The Justice Department, alongside a spate of probes across agricultural industries, has been investigating whether commercial fertilizer producers have colluded to raise prices, Bloomberg reported in March.

item.title: Generate Profitable Growth Across Your Insurance Organization with Decision Intelligence
item.description: Read Full Article The post Generate Profitable Growth Across Your Insurance Organization with Decision Intelligence appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Why Decision Intelligence is the Key to Profitable Insurance Growth The insurance industry is at a crossroads. This is the promise of Decision Intelligence: turning raw data into smarter, faster, and more transparent decisions across the entire insurance lifecycle.

item.title: Quility Honored with Two Stevie® Awards in the 2026 American Business Awards®
item.description: Quility, an award-wining insurtech company transforming insurance distribution through technology and innovation, announced it has been named the winner of two Stevie ® Awards at the 24th Annual American Business Awards ® : a Gold Stevie ® Award in the Insurance Company of the Year (Medium-Sized Company) category, and a Bronze Stevie ® Award recognizing Co-Founder Meredith Ellison in the Best Female Entrepreneur category. We congratulate this year’s Stevie Award winners and look forward to celebrating their accomplishments.” Details about The American Business Awards and the list of 2026 Stevie winners are available at ABA.StevieAwards.com. “The breadth and quality of nominations submitted to the 2026 American Business Awards reflect a dynamic and competitive business environment, where organizations are finding new ways to drive growth, deliver value, and make an impact.

item.title: Hagerty pushes deeper into agent channel
item.description: Hagerty also pointed to growth opportunities within its independent agency channel, where it works with roughly 50,000 agents, and said it is investing in straight-through processing and automated tools that help agents identify enthusiast vehicles already sitting in their existing books of business. Hagerty reported first quarter written premium growth of 18% to $289 million, as the specialty auto insurer continued expanding its State Farm partnership and completed its transition to retaining 100% of the economics on its U.S. Hagerty posted a net loss of $13 million, primarily driven by an $89 million pretax amortization charge tied to deferred ceding commissions from policies written before the January 2026 Markel fronting transition.

item.title: Oliver Wyman names AI and strategy leaders
item.description: Oliver Wyman also named Mariya Rosberg head of strategy and growth, another newly created role focused on growth priorities and the firm’s transition toward an AI-enabled operating model. Marsh’s Oliver Wyman announced three senior leadership appointments tied to its push toward AI-enabled consulting and operations. “Together, these appointments reflect a coordinated effort to reimagine how Oliver Wyman operates, pairing our industry knowledge with advanced AI capabilities to deliver greater value to clients.
